Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Person(S) Most Knowledgeable And Custodian(S) Of Records Of Intervenor American States Insurance Company (A Safeco Company) On Behalf Defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation, And To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James C. Parker of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for intervenor and opposing party American States Insurance Company.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties Gloria Mcclain, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-In-Interest to Tommy Mcclain, Deceased; Lorrie Mcclain, Wendy Spencer, Rebecca March as Legal Heir of Tommy Mcclain, Deceased.

Parties stipulated to all discovery motions to be heard simultaneously.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and the Court rules Plaintiff's Motion to Compel the Deposition of Person(s) Most Knowledgeable and Custodian(s) of Records of Intervenor American States Insurance Company (a Safeco Company) on Behalf of Defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation, and to Compel Production of Documents is denied. Plaintiff notifies the court of a correction to the exhibits numbering in the Hixson case in relation to the other discovery motion. Court asserts Exhibit C of Notice of deposition is addressed to Aladdin Heating Corporation and not to Intervenor and therefore Aladdin Heating, suspended for failure to file taxes, cannot participate in litigation. Discovery was served to Intervenor and Intervenor responded. Notice not enforceable against Corporation. Court to issue order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537, email: early.langley@outlook.com (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

Off Calendar Jul-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jon James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for Intervenor and moving party American States Insurance Company.

RULING: SPECIALLY APPEARING AMERICAN STATES INSURANCE COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION TO 1) SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND 2) GRANT LEAVE TO FILE AN ANSWER IN INTERVENTION
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially Appearing American States Insurance Company's Amended Motion to 1) Set Aside Default Judgment and 2) Grant Leave to File an Answer in Intervention is GRANTED. The Court did not consider Plaintiff's conditional non-opposition as it was untimely filed. (C.C.P. 1005(b).) The default judgment entered against Aladdin Heating Corporation on September 9, 2014 is set aside solely as related to American States Insurance Company and leave to file answer in intervention is GRANTED. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Miles Lendway, CSR#13684.

Heather-Ann Young, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs, GLORIA MCCLAN, LORRIE STEVENS, WENDY SPENCER and REBECCA MARCH ("plaintiffs")
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY in the amount of $634,342.00 in Economic Damages, $1,600,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $2,234,342.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

Trial Setting Conference
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for trialing setting conference.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Not Reported.

Jennifer Alesio, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Gloria McClain, et al.
Fourteen defense attorneys representing approximately twenty-five of the forty-five of the remaining defendants in the case are also present.

2:22 p.m. - Case is called and counsel state their respective appearances.
2:23 p.m. - At the last calling of the case plaintiffs' motion for preference was DENIED. Today's hearing is for trial setting.
2:24 p.m. - Discovery is closed, by the preference motion was denied. Expert disclosures have not been made.
2:26 - The Court informs counsel that no trial date shall be issued until expert discovery has concluded.
2:27 p.m. - Discussion between the Court and counsel regarding the status of settlement negotiations.
2:28 p.m. - The Court shall not issue a trial date until counsel have engaged in good faith settlement negotiations.
2:33 p.m. - The parties shall conclude expert discovery two Fridays prior to the Monday trial date. (i.e. if the trial call date is Monday, September 9th, then expert discovery shall conclude on Friday, August 30th).
2:37 p.m. - The Court sets this matter sets a trial date of September 9, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220.
2:38 p.m. - To date, approximately forty-five defendants remain in the case.

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calender this day for hearing on plaintiffs Gloria McClain, Lorrie Stevens, Wendy Spencer and Rebecca March's motion for preference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 36a, and for extending discovery cutoff.

Jennifer Alesio, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Gloria McClain, et al.
Ulla M. Pajala, Esq. appeared for defendant East Bay Sheet Metal Works.

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff Sloniker, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Robert Nelson, et al.
Daniel L. Martens, Esq., of DAL SAGLIO & MARTENS LLP (310-662-4748), appearing via CourtCall for defendant / opposing party Aerojet-General Corporation

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL AEROJET-GENERAL CORPORATION TO PRODUCE ITS CUSTODIAN(S) OF RECORDS, PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND REQUESTED DOCUMENTS

Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion to compel Aerojet-General Corporation to produce its custodian(s) of records, person(s) most knowledgeable and requested documents is granted in part and denied in part. The scope of the PMK deposition is limited to the dates that Mr. McClain worked at the Aerojet facility as stated in Exhibit A to the deposition notice. The COR deposition and production of documents is limited to the dates that Mr. McClain worked at the Aerojet facility, the specific locations within that facility and Mr.McClain's employers as stated in Exhibit A to the deposition notice. Defendant is ordered to investigate and provide to plaintiff any further information regarding the specified rooms. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Campbell Soup Company'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents; Requests For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Tommy McClain, et al.
James J. O'Brien, Esq., of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / opposing party Campbell Soup Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS TOMMY MCCLAIN, ET AL.'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel the depositions of defendant Campbell Soup Company's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for production of documents is denied. In light of the fact that defendant's PMK was recently deposed on some of the issues covered by the deposition notice and neither of the living product identification witnesses listed in plaintiffs' standard interrogatory responses have been made available for deposition or their contact information provided to defendant, plaintiffs have not provided good cause for their motion at the present time. Defendant's cross-motion for protective order is granted only to the extent that the motion seeks to defer the deposition sought by plaintiffs until a reasonable time after plaintiffs have made Maurice Lawrence and Mel Swanson available for deposition or provided the contact information for Mr. Lawrence and Mr. Swanson to defendant. All requests for sanctions are denied. No party acted without substantial justification. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
